rss.title: Simpler, older version of Stonehenge found three miles from famous site
rss.snippet: The structure consisted of two posts that lined up with the solstices 5,000 years ago.

rss.title: What is El Niño and why could it mean record temperatures?
rss.snippet: Global temperatures are expected to rise in the coming months as El Niño begins.

rss.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
rss.snippet: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

arxiv_papers.title: Optimal scenario design for climate emulation
arxiv_papers.snippet: As deep learning for physical systems continues to grow in popularity, efforts to improve generalizability have primarily focused on designing architectures that embed physical constraints. arxiv_papers.title: Regional Climate Model Emulation with Diffusion Approaches: What is the Added Value of Generative Machine Learning?
arxiv_papers.snippet: Emulators provide a cost-effective alternative to regional climate models (RCMs) by capturing their dynamical downscaling function. podcast: [How to feng shui your fridge — and other happy climate hacks | Jiaying Zhao (re-release)] Is it possible for taking action on climate change to make you feel happy? Behavioral scientist Jiaying Zhao believes that's the only way we'll create lasting, sustainable change. From treat meals to feng shui fridges, she offers eight life hacks to lower your carbon emissions while increasing your joy and fulfillment. Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
